figures.caption: Figure 1. Iso-contours of streamwise velocity (20% of max(𝑢𝑧) in white and min(𝑢𝑧) in black) and vorticity (10% of max(𝜔𝑧) in orange and min(𝜔𝑧) in blue) at selected times for (𝑅𝑒, 𝐸0) = (4000, 9.4 · 10−6).
Figure 2. (𝑎) The total energy gain 𝐺, the energy gain of the cross-sectional components 𝐺𝑟 , 𝜃 and the energy gain of the cross-sectional components of selected modes ∑ 𝑚 ∑ 𝑘 𝐺 (𝑚,𝑘 )

Figure 3. (𝑎) The energy gain at the end of the Orr phase, oblique wave phase and lift-up phase for NLOPs at various 𝐸0 and 𝑅𝑒 = 4000 (phases are defined and indicated in figure 2). (𝑏) The total energy gain and (𝑐) the cross-sectional component of the energy gain of the NLOP for 𝑅𝑒 = 4000, 𝐸0 = 9.35 · 10−6 over time for 𝑡 > 12 where 𝐺 (𝑡) = 𝐸 (𝑡)/𝐸 (𝑡 = 12). The dashed line corresponds to the linearized simulation and the solid line to the fully nonlinear simulation. Semi-nonlinear simulations are indicated by markers.

Figure 4. (𝑎) − ( 𝑓 ) Snapshots of 𝑢𝑧 of the NLOP at 𝑅𝑒 = 2000 and 𝑅𝑒 = 5000 in selected planes at a constant 𝜃, 𝑟 and 𝑧 corresponding to the position of maximum energy density. (𝑔) − (𝑖) Relative energy density of the NLOP with highest initial energy that still lies inside the laminar attractor as a function of 𝑟, 𝜃 and 𝑧 for different 𝑅𝑒. In the periodic directions, NLOPs are shifted so that their maximum energy lies in the centre. The insets in (𝑔) shows 𝛿𝑟 as a function of 𝑅𝑒. The dashed lines indicates a scaling of 𝑅𝑒−1/3.

Figure 5. (𝑎) The maximum energy achieved by optimal perturbation as a function of their initial energy 𝐸0 for various 𝑅𝑒. The dashed line indicates constant maximum energy 𝐸max = 0.13 ≈ 𝐸tr. (𝑏) The energy gain during the initial Orr phase and the subsequent oblique wave and lift-up phase as a function of 𝑅𝑒.

Figure 6. (𝑎) The minimal necessary energy 𝐸crit 0 as a function of 𝑅𝑒 to trigger transition to turbulence. Circles indicate NLOPs that do not trigger turbulence transition and crosses correspond to those that trigger turbulent episodes. Triangles indicate a linear fit of the minimal seed and the solid line corresponds to the instability threshold (4.5). (𝑏) Same as (𝑎) but compensated by 𝑅𝑒3 to show the deviation of the results from the scaling at small Reynolds numbers, 𝑅𝑒 ≲ 1750.
